description_long: This object is a whole shrimp, likely a tiger prawn, with a segmented, elongated body displaying a gradient of colors from pale beige to brown with subtle pinkish hues. The exoskeleton is clearly visible, with distinct banding along the abdomen and a slightly translucent quality. Numerous slender legs and swimmerets are visible beneath the body, along with two prominent claws and long, thin antennae extending from the head. The eyes are dark and set on short stalks at the front of the cephalothorax. The tail fans out at the end, showing a darker coloration and a slightly rough texture. The overall appearance is that of a fresh, uncooked crustacean, commonly found in seafood markets or urban food settings.
description: A whole, uncooked shrimp with a segmented, banded exoskeleton, visible legs, claws, and long antennae. The body displays beige, brown, and pinkish tones with a slightly translucent shell.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the shrimp, with all legs, claws, and antennae visible, and the banded exoskeleton clearly displayed.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the symmetry of the body, the arrangement of the legs, and the fan-shaped tail, with antennae extending outward.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, but with the shrimp facing the other direction, showing the same anatomical features from the other side.
description_view_3: Frontal view focusing on the head, with both eyes on stalks and the long antennae extending forward, while the legs and claws are visible beneath.
